prompt_inputs.goal: Marathon training - high-endurance athlete requiring 3000+ calories with carbohydrate and hydration focus

solution_criteria: Total daily calories meet or exceed 3000 kcal
Carbohydrates comprise 55-65% of total calories with strategic timing around training
Hydration strategy specified with timing and volume recommendations
Meal plan is compact and covers exactly one full day
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Testing with a high-endurance athlete (marathon runner) requiring 3000+ calories with emphasis on carbohydrates and hydration timing

prompt_inputs.goal: Build muscle and strength through weightlifting with optimal protein intake and pre/post-workout nutrition timing

solution_criteria: Meal plan covers full day with 4-5 meals including pre-workout and post-workout nutrition
Protein intake is 1.6-2.2g per kg of body weight (136-187g total)
Meals are timed strategically around training session with carbs/protein 1-2 hours before and within 1-2 hours after
Plan is compact and concise (suitable for quick reference)
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Testing with a strength-based athlete (weightlifter) needing protein-focused meals with specific macro ratios and meal timing around training windows

prompt_inputs.goal: Maintain energy and performance for 2 daily swimming training sessions (morning 90 min, evening 60 min)
prompt_inputs.restrictions: Severe nut allergy (no peanuts, tree nuts, or cross-contamination)

solution_criteria: Meal plan covers all 3 meals plus snacks suitable for 2 daily training sessions
All foods are nut-free with no risk of cross-contamination
Provides adequate carbohydrates and protein for swimmer's energy and recovery needs
Plan is compact and concise (suitable for 1 day reference)
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Testing with a sport-specific athlete (swimmer) having dietary restrictions (nut allergy) while maintaining energy for multiple daily training sessions
